title: Fluorospot Assay

steps: Coat plates with antibodies.
Leave plates Overnight at 4 °C .
Block plates:
Discard coating antibody and tap on paper.
Wash with 100 µL, PBS / well 3x .
Add 100 µL, culture media .
Incubate at 37 °C for 01:00:00 .
Harvest cells from culture wells (14 day expansion) with pipette (pipetting up and down), centrifuge and count. %s %s
Prepare stimuli dilutions: %s %s
Plate 50 µL /well each of stimuli and PBMCs.
Incubate plates for 24:00:00 at 37 °C, , 5% CO2 .
Plate layout: %s %s
Remove the cells by emptying the plate and wash 5 times with 200 µL, PBS /well (plate washer).
Dilute the detection antibodies, per full plate: NEW SYSTEM %s %s
Add 100 µL /well and incubate for 02:00:00 at Room temperature in the dark.
Wash 5 times with 200 µL, PBS /well.
Dilute the fluorophores, per full plate: NEW SYSTEM %s %s
Incubate at Room temperature in the dark for 01:00:00 .
Wash 5 times with 200 µL, PBS /well.
Empty the plate and add 50 µL, Fluorescence enhancer-II /well and leave the plate for 00:15:00 at Room temperature .
Empty the plate and remove residual Fluorescence enhancer by firmly tapping the plate against clean paper towels. Do not wash in the sink.
Remove the underdrain (the soft plastic under the plate).
Perform all work in laminar flow hood.
Coat plate with 50 µL, antibody diluted in PBS per well (according to table below): %s %s
Discard MeOH and wash plate 3x with 100 µL, sterile water .
Prewet plates with 50 µL, 70% MeOH / well
Use IPFL for Fluorospot (Mabtech).
Leave the plate in the dark to dry; plate should be completely dry before analysis.
Inspect and count spots in a Fluorospot reader.
Store plate in the dark at Room temperature .
creator: Ngan Kim Tran
